steps.title: Identify the contrast
steps.content: The passage establishes that cheetahs and blue whales were charismatic according to participants' responses, but then contrasts this by stating no animal outranked the tiger. This indicates a shift from listing specific animals to highlighting the tiger's higher ranking.

steps.title: Match the transition word to the contrast
steps.content:
• Choice A — Still, uses a contrast transition (such as however or nevertheless) that signals the shift from naming charismatic animals to stating that no animal outranked the tiger. This fits the passage's move from "cheetahs and blue whales were charismatic" to "no animal outranked the tiger." ✓
  • Choice B — As a result, signals cause and effect, but the tiger's ranking is not presented as a result of the cheetah and blue whale landing in the top twenty. ✗
  • Choice C — In other words, signals restatement, but the tiger's top ranking is a new ranking detail, not a restatement of the previous sentence. ✗
  • Choice D — For example, uses an example transition such as for example, suggesting the tiger is merely one illustration of the charismatic animals already mentioned, rather than the standout that outranked them all. ✗
That is the correct answer.
